briefing: As of early March 24, 2026, global markets are reacting to the geopolitical tension in the Middle East, particularly the U.S.-Iran situation. Following the KOSPI's 6.49% plunge on March 23, markets remain highly volatile. While former President Trump announced a 5-day pause in military strikes, Iran has denied any negotiations are occurring. Analysts, including those from JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs, suggest the pullback could be a long-term buying opportunity, citing semiconductor strength, though elevated uncertainty persists.
